author: @curtstacy779
text: Making your own tools is great. just be careful when you start making powered tools. lol. when they go wrong they can really go wrong.

author: @davidfuller764
text: Heck yes I like in subscribe to this one and everyone

replies.author: @AndreaDingbatt
replies.text:
Hello!! Great channel here!!
Brilliant folks and much to learn!!

I'm having to relearn so much, and Rob is Amazing,
 always gets the brains working, and keen for us to give it a go!!

I'm often thinking that if Rob had been a Teacher at my school, I would have retained the info, 
and been thinking for myself!!

Kind Regards,
Andrea and Critter Family. XxX

author: @AndreaDingbatt
text:
Hi Rob, I hope all is well with you and yours.🥰
Was the app thing any use at all?!
I'm kind of guessing maybe not?
Kind regards,
A. Dingbatt. XxX

author: @____________________________.x
text: It’s always interesting to see antique tools, because a lot of them were made by the Engineer who owned them. I think I’ve made a few myself but only a pair of external calipers comes to mind. My grandad made a lovely pair of parallel jaw pliers

replies.author: @AndreaDingbatt
replies.text:
They are things of Beauty!!
And lasted a few generations too, I hate seeing the throw away society,
So pleased it's changing for the better!!

author: @gyrgrls
text: I'd love to make my own tools,  but I lack the necessary tools.

author: @ruthcormack1765
text: I've always found circlips the most irritating fixer of all my life. Like springs I often get them flying into mini black holes. I like cotter pins a lot better.

replies.author: @____________________________.x
replies.text: I personally love ballbearings with a hidden spring, that spontaneously disassemble themselves if you so much as look at them funny

replies.author: @AndreaDingbatt
replies.text:
@____________________________.x Oh, Tell me about Them!!
PiiiyyyoooooiiiiinnnnnG!!
(Spatter, Patter, Patter, ping, Damn!!)
I'm glad I am Not Alone in this, and, it was Always on something that was Essential!! Lol!

I hope That the WRAF Know just how Lucky they were, when they avoided "The IKEA Effect,"!! 

(* As my Dad calls me!)
I had to leave, and take care of my mother for a while....
I Was, 
~ an Apprentice Aircraft Engineer, Lmao!! 

And I am certain my "Gift" to those Brave Pilots, 
who could have been flying Anything that I had touched, Wow!

The Lives I Saved, by Leaving!! 
Kindest Regards, 
Andrea and Critter Family. XxX
Ps, I Love your profile name!!

replies.author: @____________________________.x
replies.text: @AndreaDingbatt heh, I think the last thing that was "never the same again" was my nice tape measure. Oh, and a multimeter which used to work, but I hadn't realised that the contacts on the rotating dial would just fall apart if you opened it. And it wasn't obvious which way they all go back, so now it only reads voltage, sigh... :/

replies.author: @AndreaDingbatt
replies.text:
@____________________________.x Oops!!
"The IKEA Effect"
maybe you have it as well?! 
Perhaps we are related! Hehehee!! 

I hope you are well, and haven't been close to any Aircraft, or Boats /Ships/Submarines, Lol!!

Thank You so much for having me giggling, laughter is a Gift!!
Namaste 🙏
Andrea and Critter Family. XxX

replies.author: @____________________________.x
replies.text: @AndreaDingbatt I did crash a rather important IT system once by pressing typing the wrong thing, but no kittens were harmed and it kinda fixed itself after a reboot. I think that's the only thing I've done that affected a lot of people 😬 The best thing was that nobody realised it was me... 🤐

author: @jeffreyrood8755
text: Yep making tools is a necessity. Used to have to make them a lot more than I do now working on ATVs and motorcycles. Wasn't a big deal to make a tool that you needed or adapt to another tool. Great video Thank you for sharing
